name: Low Cal Marbled Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan over medium-high heat, bring 2/3 cup milk to a boil; set aside.
2. In a blender container, sprinkle gelatin over the remaining 1/4 cup cold skim milk and let stand for 4 to 5 minutes.
3. Add the boiling skim milk; cover and blend at low speed for 2 minutes.
4. Add 6 tablespoons sugar, egg yolks, and vanilla; blend at medium speed until well mixed.
5. Add cottage cheese; blend at high speed until smooth. Remove 3/4 cup of this mixture to use for marbling and chill until the mixture begins to thicken.
6. In a small bowl, combine 1 tablespoon sugar and cocoa; stir until evenly blended.
7. Pour the chocolate mixture into a large bowl. Chill, stirring occasionally, until the mixture mounds when dropped from a spoon.
8. In a small bowl with an electric mixer at high speed, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually add the remaining 6 tablespoons sugar; beat until stiff peaks form.
9. Fold the beaten egg whites into the chocolate mixture and pour into the prepared crust.
10. Spoon large dollops of vanilla mixture over the chocolate; gently swirl with a knife or spatula to create a marbled effect.
11. Cover and refrigerate until firm, about 4 to 5 hours.
12. CHOCOLATE CRUMB CRUST: In a small bowl, combine crumbs and cocoa; add melted margarine and stir until evenly blended. Press the mixture evenly on the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan.
